title: REM universality and Poisson-Dirichlet Gibbs weights for linear random energy
abstract: We study the Hamiltonian $H_n(h,σ)=\sum_{i=1}^n h_i(σ_i-m), $ where $(h_i)$ are i.i.d.\ real random variables and $(σ_i)$ are i.i.d.\ Ising spins. We consider the energy levels obtained after an independent thinning that retains an exponential number of configurations ($e^{O(n)}$). We prove that, after an $(h_i)$-dependent centering, the resulting point process converges in distribution to a Poisson point process with exponential intensity. Thus, the energy levels asymptotically has the one of the Random Energy Model (REM). Our results extend previous ones, where REM universality for this model was established only either for energy fluctuations of order $e^{-O(n)}$ or for $e^{o(\sqrt n)}$ randomly selected configurations. We also identify the limiting Gibbs weights, which converge to a Poisson--Dirichlet law, and the quenched free energy, which exhibits a freezing transition at $β=\tildeλ$. The proofs are presented here in compressed form; full details are given in the companion preprint.
summary: This paper proves that the energy levels of a purely linear random Hamiltonian exhibit Random Energy Model (REM) universality after thinning. It rigorously determines that the resulting point process converges to a Poisson Point Process and identifies that the associated Gibbs weights converge to the Poisson-Dirichlet law, revealing a phase transition in the free energy at a critical temperature.
main_problem: The study aims to establish Random Energy Model (REM) universality for energy levels derived from a purely linear random Hamiltonian and determine the limiting behavior of associated Gibbs weights.
main_result: The thinned point process of energy levels converges to a Poisson Point Process, and the joint distribution of ranked Gibbs weights converges to the Poisson-Dirichlet law in the low-temperature regime.
method: The analysis uses techniques involving independent thinning processes, computing limit Laplace functionals for point processes, and applying large-deviation principles to analyze weight convergence.
model_or_system: The system is defined by the Hamiltonian H_n(h, \sigma) = \sum_{i=1}^n h_i(\sigma_i - m), where h_i are i.i.d. random variables and \sigma_i are Ising spins. The analysis focuses on energy levels obtained after retaining an exponential number of configurations.
key_observables: Energy level point process, Gibbs weights, quenched free energy F(β).
important_parameters: β (inverse temperature), τλ (critical parameter related to the PPP intensity), and the thinning factor c_ό.
main_assumptions: The proof relies on regularity hypotheses for h_1, including a linear small-ball condition, and assumes sharp large-deviation estimates from prior work.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the quenched large-deviation rate function G*(a) versus a, defining parameters τλ and α.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes REM universality for the point process convergence to PPP(D_{τλ}), then analyzes the Gibbs weights showing convergence to PD(τλ/β, 0), and finally computes the free energy exhibiting a phase transition at β = τλ.
why_interesting: The rigorous proof of REM universality for this specific linear model provides deep insights into how complex statistical physics models simplify to universal limiting forms, which is relevant for understanding disordered systems in quantum mechanics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
